sentences.chinese: 齊悼惠王劉肥者，高祖長庶男也。
sentences.literal: King Daohui of Qi, Liu Fei, was Gaozu's eldest son by a concubine.
His mother was a concubine surnamed Cao.

sentences.chinese: 高祖六年，立肥為齊王，食七十城，諸民能齊言者皆予齊王。
sentences.literal: In Gaozu year 6, he established Fei as King of Qi, ruling over seventy cities, and all people who could speak the Qi dialect were given to the King of Qi.
The King of Qi was Emperor Hui's elder brother.
In Emperor Hui's second year, the King of Qi entered the capital for audience.

sentences.chinese: 惠帝與齊王燕飲，亢禮如家人。
sentences.literal: Emperor Hui feasted with the King of Qi, treating each other with the familiarity of family.
Empress Lü was angry and intended to execute the King of Qi.

sentences.chinese: 齊王懼不得脫，乃用其內史勳計，獻城陽郡，以為魯元公主湯沐邑。
sentences.literal: The King of Qi feared he could not escape, so he used his interior minister Xun's plan, offering Chengyang commandery as a bathing fief for Princess Lu Yuan.
Empress Lü was pleased, and thus he was able to take leave and return to his kingdom.

sentences.chinese: 悼惠王即位十三年，以惠帝六年卒。
sentences.literal: King Daohui reigned for thirteen years and died in Emperor Hui's sixth year.
His son Xiang ascended the throne, becoming King Ai.

sentences.chinese: 哀王元年，孝惠帝崩，呂太后稱制，天下事皆決於高后。
sentences.literal: In King Ai's first year, Emperor Hui passed away. Empress Lü acted as regent, and all affairs of the realm were decided by the empress dowager.

sentences.chinese: 二年，高后立其兄子酈侯呂臺為呂王，割齊之濟南郡為呂王奉邑。
sentences.literal: In year 2, the empress dowager established her brother's son Marquis Lü Tai of Lü as King of Lü, carving out Jinan commandery from Qi as the King of Lü's fief.

sentences.chinese: 哀王三年，其弟章入宿衛於漢，呂太后封為朱虛侯，以呂祿女妻之。
sentences.literal: In King Ai's third year, his younger brother Zhang entered Han's palace guard service. Empress Lü enfeoffed him as Marquis of Zhuxu and married him to Lü Lü's daughter.

sentences.chinese: 後四年，封章弟興居為東牟侯，皆宿衛長安中。
sentences.literal: Four years later, she enfeoffed Zhang's younger brother Xingju as Marquis of Dongmu. Both served as palace guards in Chang'an.
